description: You are a DevOps/security engineer at DevLabs. The security team has informed you that the SSL certificate issue for api.devlabs.io — previously marked 'Resolved' — had incomplete remediation. The certbot cronjob was never actually verified to run correctly after the permission fix, and there is now evidence the renewal will fail again. Handle this security incident in the Infrastructure & DevOps project by reopening the 'SSL certificate for api.devlabs.io expires in 14 days' issue with status 'In Progress', priority 'Immediate', and the comment: 'REOPENED: Auto-renewal cronjob verification was skipped. Certificate at risk. Immediate remediation required.' Create a new Bug issue (Subject: 'Certbot cronjob silent failure: renewal verification not monitored', Category: 'Security', Priority: 'High', Assigned to: 'carol.santos', Target version: 'Q1 2025 Goals', Estimated hours: '4.0', Description: 'The certbot renewal cronjob has been silently failing for over a month without any alerting. Required actions: (1) add cron job output logging to syslog, (2) set up alerting on cert expiry < 30 days via monitoring dashboard, (3) add cert expiry check to weekly infrastructure health report.') and reference its ID in a follow-up comment on the original issue. Log 2.0 hours of Development activity on the original issue with the comment: 'Investigated auto-renewal failure and documented remediation plan in follow-up issue.' Login credentials: admin / Admin1234!
